title: Medical Scientists, Except Epidemiologists
description: Conduct research dealing with the understanding of human diseases and the improvement of human health. Engage in clinical investigation, research and development, or other related activities.

tasks.text: Follow strict safety procedures when handling toxic materials to avoid contamination.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Evaluate effects of drugs, gases, pesticides, parasites, and microorganisms at various levels.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Plan and direct studies to investigate human or animal disease, preventive methods, and treatments for disease.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare and analyze organ, tissue, and cell samples to identify toxicity, bacteria, or microorganisms or to study cell structure.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Conduct research to develop methodologies, instrumentation, and procedures for medical application, analyzing data and presenting findings to the scientific audience and general public.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Teach principles of medicine and medical and laboratory procedures to physicians, residents, students, and technicians.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Write and publish articles in scientific journals.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Standardize drug dosages, methods of immunization, and procedures for manufacture of drugs and medicinal compounds.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Study animal and human health and physiological processes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Investigate cause, progress, life cycle, or mode of transmission of diseases or parasites.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Use equipment such as atomic absorption spectrometers, electron microscopes, flow cytometers, or chromatography systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with health departments, industry personnel, physicians, and others to develop health safety standards and public health improvement programs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Consult with and advise physicians, educators, researchers, and others regarding medical applications of physics, biology, and chemistry.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
